text: My jobs requires wearing suits. That is why I have it in several different colors and different materials. My favorite one is an elegant slim black english wool suit. It is also very light. text: When I was 18 years old my mother learned me to drive a car and I drove many hours with her. The 2nd december 1986 at 11.00 A.M., I passed my driving licence and I was so happy to obtain it. At 1.00 P.M. the same day I took, for the first time, the car alone, I turned on my left and a car was parking on the street I waited patiently behind it. I don't know why yet today but man in the car in front of me put the walking rear on and broke completely my car. I think it has been the most quick accident in my city.
comment (human): “Obtain” is grammatically correct but too formal and stiff for a personal story; “get” makes it sound natural and human.

text: I arrive at work at 9h00 (I put 20 minutes on the way). I began to check my mails. If i receive requests for action, I move among users for help. I lunch at 12h30 and resume work at 13h30. I spend the afternoon if the activity is quiet to repair computers. I left work at 17h00.
comment (human): “Receive” is grammatically correct but slightly formal for describing a daily routine; “get” is more natural in casual, neutral writing.

text: I always see to big department stores, but I often buy to small shops. Small shops are more friendly and sales assistants are there for to recommend something. I very rarely shop online, because I'm not sure to receive my order quickly, yet it's better cheap.
comment (human): “Receive” is correct grammatically, but in informal, personal writing like an online profile, “get” (the order sent) is more natural and conversational.

text: There was a theft ten years ago just in front of my own house ! Our camping-car was stolen during a night ; nobody had heart the thieves, neither my oldest son who was asleep in his room, neither the neighbors. The day after this night, my husband has informed the police ; but it was impossible to refind the car.
comment (human): Inform’ is correct and appropriate in formal or official contexts, but since this is supposed to be a personal story or even a party invitation the whole text is completely off-topic and the word choice highlights the mismatch.

text: To: xxx@orange.fr xxx@orange.fr Subject: Paris networking event. Hi Adele and Yves Yesterday, I took part in a networking event in Paris. There were about fifty of retirement houses' managers. Their purpose was to share a modern computer network. They were very interested by our innovative product. I am confident that they will call me for making appointments. I 'll inform you about developpemts of this deal. Just give me a call if you have any questions. Pierre.
comment (human): “Inform” is correct and fits the formal/professional tone expected in a resume or business communication. Casual alternatives like “tell” or “let you know” would be inappropriate here.

text: I arrive at work at 9h00 (I put 20 minutes on the way). I began to check my mails. If i receive requests for action, I move among users for help. I lunch at 12h30 and resume work at 13h30. I spend the afternoon if the activity is quiet to repair computers. I left work at 17h00.
comment (human): Receive’ sounds unnatural in this informal, storytelling context. A more natural word would be ‘get’, or you could use ‘be given’ to keep the meaning clear.
